name: Chestnut-Mushroom Casserole
recipeInstructions:
1. Prepare a white sauce by melting the butter in a saucepan.
2. Add flour to the melted butter and stir until smooth.
3. Gradually whisk in the half-and-half, adding it slowly to prevent lumps.
4. Dissolve vegetable bouillon powder in a small amount of hot water.
5. Stir the dissolved bouillon into the white sauce mixture.
6. Season with salt and white pepper.
7. Sauté the mushrooms in a bit of butter until they begin to brown.
8. Add the sautéed mushrooms, chopped chestnuts, and mushroom soup can contents to the saucepan.
9. Transfer the mixture to a greased casserole dish.
10. Sprinkle cheddar cheese evenly over the top.
11. Place the casserole in a preheated oven at 350°F (175°C).
12. Bake for 20-30 minutes, or until the cheese is melted and bubbly.
13. Serve hot.
(Note: This recipe yields a large portion suitable for sharing with company or as part of a covered dish meal. For individual serving sizes, prepare two casserole dishes and freeze one after baking. Thaw completely before reheating.)
